meta_title: Roofing in North Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in North Lauderdale, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, 500+ homes. Free estimate!
h1: North Lauderdale's Most Trusted Roofing Contractor — Built for South Florida Storms
intro:
If you own a home in North Lauderdale, your roof is doing serious work every single year. Positioned in the heart of Broward County, this tight-knit community of approximately 45,000 residents sits roughly eight miles from the Atlantic coast — close enough to feel every tropical system that pushes through South Florida, yet far enough inland that many homeowners underestimate just how vulnerable their rooftops really are. With a 170 mph design wind speed requirement and a storm history that includes nine named storms since 1990, the question for North Lauderdale homeowners is never if their roof will be tested — it's when. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' brings the expertise, products, and care your home demands.

Neighborhoods like North Lauderdale Village and Ramblewood are home to a diverse mix of single-family homes, many of which were built before the stricter post-Andrew building codes took effect in 1994. In fact, nearly half — 48 percent — of North Lauderdale's housing stock predates those code revisions, meaning tens of thousands of rooftops in this ZIP code (33068) may be working with outdated fastening systems, inadequate underlayment, or shingles simply not rated for today's wind load standards. With average home values hovering around $290,000, protecting your investment with a properly engineered, code-compliant roof replacement isn't a luxury — it's a financial necessity.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've completed more than 500 roof replacements and repairs across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the specific challenges that North Lauderdale homes present. From the flat-lot drainage issues near Hampton Pines Park to the older concrete tile roofs common throughout Ramblewood, our team brings local knowledge that generic contractors simply don't have. Whether you need a full replacement with CertainTeed architectural shingles or a premium West Lake concrete tile system, we deliver craftsmanship backed by 60-plus years of combined experience — with every installation handled entirely in-house.

section_why.h2: Why North Lauderdale Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Project
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Florida is one of the most consequential decisions a homeowner can make, and in a city like North Lauderdale, the stakes are especially high. Too many contractors operate as middlemen — they sell you a roof, then subcontract the actual installation to whoever is available. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't work that way. Every single roof we install is completed by our own trained, background-checked crews. No subcontractors, no surprises, no accountability gaps. That commitment to in-house installation is one of the reasons we've maintained an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and earned the trust of homeowners from Lauderdale Lakes to Margate and everywhere in between.

Our roofing team brings more than 60 years of combined hands-on experience to every project. We understand Florida's roofing environment in ways that out-of-state storm chasers never will — the way afternoon thunderstorms accelerate deck deterioration, how South Florida's UV index degrades lower-grade shingles faster than manufacturers predict, and why proper nail pattern compliance under Broward County's code isn't just a technicality, it's the difference between a roof that survives a hurricane and one that peels away in 100 mph winds. When we assess your North Lauderdale home, we're not just looking at surface wear — we're evaluating your decking, fascia, soffit, and drainage before we ever write a proposal.

We're also deeply familiar with the permitting requirements specific to North Lauderdale. We handle every step of the City of North Lauderdale Building Division permit process on your behalf — from application and plan submission to scheduling the required inspections. You never have to navigate city hall yourself. This level of service is part of what it means to be The Kings of Service. When the job is done, you receive a complete permit package, manufacturer documentation, and wind mitigation report — everything you need to file with your insurance carrier and start saving money immediately.

For homeowners in the lower-middle income range that characterizes much of North Lauderdale, we also know that budget matters. That's why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified customers. A new hurricane-rated roof doesn't have to break the bank, and with the right financing structure, the monthly cost is often offset quickly by reductions in your homeowner's insurance premium.

section_history.h2: The Storms That Shaped North Lauderdale — And What They Mean for Your Roof Today
section_history.body:
North Lauderdale's storm history reads like a crash course in South Florida roofing vulnerability. Hurricane Andrew made landfall in August 1992 as a Category 5 storm and, while its most catastrophic damage was concentrated further south in Miami-Dade County, Broward County sustained widespread roof damage that exposed a generation of building failures. Thousands of homes in communities just like North Lauderdale lost shingles, fascia boards, and entire roof sections — not because the storm was unpredictable, but because the construction standards of the time were woefully inadequate. Andrew is the reason Florida rewrote its building code, and it's the reason nearly half of all pre-1994 homes in North Lauderdale are considered high-risk by today's standards.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 brought a different kind of destruction to Broward County. As a rapidly moving storm, Wilma produced sustained winds that battered rooftops with an extended duration that overwhelmed older shingle systems. Neighborhoods throughout North Lauderdale reported widespread granule loss, lifted shingles, and damaged ridge caps — the kind of incremental damage that doesn't always result in an immediate insurance claim but quietly accelerates roof deterioration for years afterward. Many of those same roofs are now approaching the end of their service life, making proactive replacement more urgent than ever.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a stark reminder that no community in South Florida is immune, even when a storm's eyewall tracks away from Broward County. Irma's wind field was so expansive that North Lauderdale experienced tropical storm and hurricane-force gusts for hours, causing widespread damage to aging rooftops throughout the 33068 ZIP code. With nine named storms impacting the area since 1990, the historical data is clear: North Lauderdale homeowners who invest in properly rated, code-compliant roofing systems experience dramatically less storm damage and far fewer costly claims over time.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Roofing Requirements Every North Lauderdale Homeowner Should Know
section_code.body:
North Lauderdale is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which means it falls under a slightly different regulatory framework than Miami-Dade and parts of coastal Broward. However, do not mistake 'non-HVHZ' for 'lower standard.' The Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated roofing products throughout Broward County, and North Lauderdale's 170 mph design wind speed places it firmly in a category requiring robust, tested roofing assemblies. Every product we install — whether CertainTeed architectural shingles or West Lake concrete tile — carries the Florida Product Approval documentation necessary to satisfy Broward County's strict inspection protocols.

For roofing projects in North Lauderdale, permits are required for all full replacements and most significant repairs. The City of North Lauderdale Building Division processes roofing permits and schedules the mandatory inspections that verify compliance with current code. This includes verification of the nail pattern, underlayment type, and proper installation of drip edge and ice and water shield in vulnerable areas. Our team manages this entire process — from initial permit application to the final passed inspection — so that you are never left wondering about your project's legal status. We have a long-standing working relationship with local inspectors and understand exactly what documentation and installation techniques they require.

One of the most important code considerations for older North Lauderdale homes is the requirement to bring existing roofs up to current standards during replacement. If your home was built before 1994, the deck attachment, ventilation, and flashings may all need to be upgraded to meet today's Florida Building Code. We perform a thorough pre-installation assessment on every project to identify these requirements upfront, so there are no surprise change orders mid-project. Transparency in code compliance is a cornerstone of the Premier Impact Windows & Roofing approach, and it's why our completed projects consistently pass inspection on the first visit.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles and West Lake Tile — Premium Roofing Systems Built for North Lauderdale Homes
section_products.body:
When it comes to roofing products, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing doesn't cut corners. We've carefully selected our product lines based on performance in South Florida's demanding climate — and both of our flagship brands have earned their reputations through years of proven results in hurricane-prone communities across Broward County.

For homeowners seeking a high-quality shingle solution, we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles. The Landmark series features a dual-layer construction that delivers superior wind resistance, rated to withstand winds up to 130 mph with proper installation — and when installed with enhanced fastening patterns per Florida code, this system performs even better in high-wind events. CertainTeed shingles also carry a Class A fire rating and come with a StreakFighter algae-resistance treatment, which is critical in North Lauderdale's humid subtropical climate where black streaking from algae growth is a common cosmetic and structural concern. The Landmark series is available in a wide range of colors and profiles, making it an excellent choice for homeowners in North Lauderdale Village and Ramblewood who want durable, attractive curb appeal without a premium tile price point.

For homeowners who prefer the look and longevity of tile, we install West Lake concrete and clay tile roofing systems. West Lake tile is manufactured to Florida Product Approval standards and engineered specifically for high-wind environments. Concrete tile, in particular, offers outstanding thermal mass — helping to moderate attic temperatures and reduce cooling costs, which is a meaningful benefit in a South Florida summer. Clay tile provides a beautiful, traditional Mediterranean aesthetic that adds lasting value to higher-end North Lauderdale homes. Both West Lake options are available in a variety of profiles and colorways, and both are installed by our in-house crews using the manufacturer-approved methods that preserve warranty coverage and satisfy Broward County inspectors.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in North Lauderdale — And How Do You Make It Work for Your Budget?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in South Florida vary widely depending on roof size, slope, material choice, and the condition of the existing deck. For a typical single-family home in North Lauderdale — where average home values sit around $290,000 and homes tend to range from 1,200 to 2,200 square feet — a full roof replacement with CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingles generally falls in the range of $9,000 to $16,000 before any insurance involvement. West Lake tile systems, which carry a higher material cost and require more intensive installation, typically range from $14,000 to $25,000 depending on tile profile and roof complexity. These are investments, but they are investments with measurable returns.

A new hurricane-rated roof in Broward County directly impacts your homeowner's insurance premium. Wind mitigation credits in Florida can reduce the wind portion of your policy by 20 to 45 percent annually, depending on your carrier and the specific attributes of your new roof — including hip roof geometry, enhanced fastening, and sealed roof deck coverage. For many North Lauderdale homeowners paying $3,000 to $5,000 or more per year in insurance, those savings can amount to $800 to $2,000 annually. Over a 10-year period, that's a significant offset against the upfront cost of your roof replacement.

For homeowners who need to manage the upfront investment, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. Financing a new roof means you don't have to delay necessary work while your existing roof continues to deteriorate and expose your home to damage. Given that North Lauderdale is a community where many residents are balancing budgets carefully, we structured our financing options specifically to make premium roofing accessible — not just aspirational. Contact us for a free, no-obligation roof assessment and customized quote, and we'll walk you through every cost and financing scenario that fits your situation.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in North Lauderdale Can Significantly Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premium
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in South Florida is among the most expensive in the nation, and Broward County policyholders know this reality all too well. Between market exits by major carriers, rising reinsurance costs, and the persistent hurricane risk that defines life in the region, annual premiums have climbed sharply over the past decade. For North Lauderdale homeowners, one of the most effective tools for controlling insurance costs is investing in a qualifying roof replacement — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps you maximize every available credit.

Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts through wind mitigation credits, and a new hurricane-rated roof is one of the most impactful ways to earn those credits. Following your roof installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides a completed wind mitigation inspection report — sometimes called a 1802 form — that documents the specific attributes of your new roof system. These attributes include roof covering type (FBC-approved shingles or tile), roof deck attachment method (typically 8d nails at 6-inch spacing under current code), and roof-to-wall connection type. When submitted to your insurance carrier, this report triggers discounts that can meaningfully reduce the wind portion of your premium, which typically makes up the majority of a South Florida homeowner's policy cost.

Many insurers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance — apply substantial wind mitigation discounts for homes with newer, code-compliant roof systems. Some carriers also distinguish between roof shapes, offering higher discounts for hip roofs versus gable-end configurations. Our team can advise you on which roofing decisions during your project may further optimize your wind mitigation score. The combination of insurance savings, increased home value, and reduced exposure to storm damage makes a new roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing one of the smartest financial decisions a North Lauderdale homeowner can make.

faqs.q: How do I know if my North Lauderdale home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: The age of your roof is the first factor to consider — if it's over 20 years old, especially on a pre-1994 home in North Lauderdale, a full replacement is often more cost-effective than ongoing repairs. Other indicators include widespread granule loss in gutters, multiple areas of lifted or missing shingles, soft spots in the deck, or interior water intrusion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free on-site assessments to give you an honest, professional evaluation with no pressure to replace if repair is the right call.

faqs.q: What does roofing financing look like, and do I need good credit to qualify?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available. We work with multiple lending partners to accommodate a range of credit profiles, including options for homeowners with less-than-perfect credit. During your free consultation, our team will walk you through available financing programs and help you identify the best fit for your monthly budget. Getting a hurricane-rated roof should not be delayed due to financing concerns — we're here to find a solution.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in North Lauderdale, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements in North Lauderdale, processed through the City of North Lauderdale Building Division. The permit ensures your project is inspected for compliance with the Florida Building Code, including correct nail patterns, underlayment, and product approvals. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — application, plan submission, and inspection scheduling. You will never have to navigate the permit office yourself. All work is fully documented and code-compliant upon completion.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical North Lauderdale home?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on single-family homes in North Lauderdale are completed in one to three days, depending on roof size, pitch, and material type. Shingle roofs typically move faster than tile installations. The permitting process, which we handle for you, generally takes one to two weeks before work can begin. Our project managers will give you a clear timeline at the start of your project and keep you informed of any changes. We work efficiently without cutting corners, respecting your home and daily routine.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my insurance premium, and by how much?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for qualifying roof systems, and a new code-compliant roof in Broward County can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 20 to 45 percent depending on your carrier and roof characteristics. For a homeowner paying $4,000 annually in insurance, that could translate to $800 to $1,800 in yearly savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides a completed wind mitigation report after every roof installation to help you submit for credits immediately.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your North Lauderdale Home With a Roof Built to Last?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof assessment — our expert team is ready to inspect your home, walk you through your options, and provide a transparent quote with financing that fits your budget.
